topic: Transformer architecture improvements in large language models 2020-2024
final_review:
The Transformer architecture has undergone significant improvements in recent years, particularly in the context of large language models (LLMs) (Gao et al., 2023). One of the key areas of research has been the development of more efficient and scalable models. Another approach has been to augment LLMs with external knowledge sources, such as chemistry tools (Bran et al., 2024) or tabular foundation models (Hollmann et al., 2025).

Retrieval-augmented generation has also been explored as a means of improving the performance of LLMs (Gao et al., 2023). This approach involves using external knowledge sources to inform the generation process, and has been shown to be effective in a variety of tasks (Gao et al., 2023). Explainability has also become an increasingly important consideration in the development of LLMs, with researchers seeking to develop more transparent and interpretable models (Zhao et al., 2024).

In addition to these technical advancements, there has been a growing interest in applying LLMs to real-world problems, such as medical image segmentation (Wang et al., 2022) and clinical text summarization (Van Veen et al., 2024). For example, researchers have developed LLMs that can outperform medical experts in clinical text summarization tasks (Van Veen et al., 2024). Autonomous agents based on LLMs have also been explored, with applications in areas such as healthcare (Wang et al., 2024) and hospitals (Maleki Varnosfaderani et al., 2024).

Despite these advancements, there are still several research gaps and challenges that need to be addressed. For example, the integration of LLMs with other AI technologies, such as graph neural networks (Silva et al., 2024), is an area of ongoing research. Furthermore, the application of LLMs to real-world problems requires careful consideration of issues such as explainability (Zhao et al., 2024) and trustworthiness (Holter et al., 2018).

In conclusion, the Transformer architecture has undergone significant improvements in recent years, driven by advances in areas such as efficiency, scalability, and explainability. The application of LLMs to real-world problems has also become an increasingly important area of research, with potential applications in areas such as healthcare and hospitals. However, there are still several research gaps and challenges that need to be addressed, including the integration of LLMs with other AI technologies.

References:

Gao, Y., Xiong, Y., & Gao, X. (2023). Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey. https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2312.10997
Hollmann, N., Müller, S., & Purucker, L. (2025). Accurate predictions on small data with a tabular foundation model. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-024-08328-6

Silva, G., Rodrigues, M., & Teixeira, A. (2024). Targeted Branching for the Maximum Independent Set Problem Using Graph Neural Networks. https://doi.org/10.4230/lipics.sea.2024.20
